title: Monsoon Mediterranean Rice Pilaf
description: A malty, crumbly monsoon-inspired Mediterranean dish featuring seasoned rice vinegar and an ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly nimono-simmer, flavored with aromatic spices and herbs, served with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Sauté the small onion and 2 cloves of garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the 1-inch piece of ginger and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the 2 tablespoons of tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the turmeric, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, and salt, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the 2 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Add 1 cup of basmati rice and stir to coat the rice with the spice mixture.
Add 2 cups of nimono dashi broth and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is cooked.
Fluff the rice with a fork and stir in 2 tablespoons of toasted sesame seeds and 1/4 cup of chopped fresh parsley.
Season with 2 tablespoons of seasoned rice vinegar and serve hot.
Serve the Monsoon Mediterranean Rice Pilaf in individual portions, garnished with additional chopped parsley and toasted sesame seeds if desired.
